explanations.tiny: Knives are very sharp and can hurt you. Only grown-ups use knives.
explanations.junior: Kitchen knives have very sharp blades that can cause serious cuts. They should only be handled by adults or older children with adult supervision.

explanations.tiny: A spoon is safe to use! We use it to eat our food.
explanations.junior: Spoons are safe eating utensils with no sharp edges. They are great for eating soups, cereals, and other foods.

explanations.tiny: The stove gets very hot and can burn you. Ask a grown-up for help.
explanations.junior: Stove burners reach very high temperatures and can cause severe burns. Children should never use the stove without adult supervision.

explanations.tiny: A cup is safe! We drink water and juice from cups.
explanations.junior: Cups are safe containers used for drinking. They are made of plastic, glass, or ceramic and are safe when handled carefully.

explanations.tiny: The blender has sharp spinning blades inside. Only grown-ups can use it.
explanations.junior: Blenders contain very sharp rotating blades that spin at high speed. They must only be operated by adults to avoid serious injuries.

explanations.tiny: A fork helps us eat our food. It is safe when we are careful.
explanations.junior: Forks are eating utensils that are safe to use when handled properly. Avoid poking yourself or others with the tines.

explanations.tiny: The microwave makes food very hot. Always ask a grown-up for help.
explanations.junior: Microwaves heat food using radiation and can make containers extremely hot. Metal objects should never go inside, and children need adult supervision to use one safely.

explanations.tiny: An oven mitt keeps your hands safe from hot things.
explanations.junior: Oven mitts are thick protective gloves that shield your hands from heat. They are a safety tool used when handling hot pots, pans, or baking trays.

explanations.tiny: Medicine is only for sick people and grown-ups give it to you. Never touch it alone.
explanations.junior: Medicine bottles contain drugs that can be dangerous if taken in the wrong amount or by the wrong person. Only adults should handle and administer medicine.

explanations.tiny: A toothbrush keeps your teeth clean and healthy!
explanations.junior: Toothbrushes are safe personal hygiene tools used to clean your teeth and gums twice a day to prevent cavities.

explanations.tiny: A razor is very sharp and only for grown-ups. Never touch it!
explanations.junior: Razors have extremely sharp blades designed for adults to shave. Children should never handle razors as they can cause deep cuts.

explanations.tiny: Soap cleans your hands and keeps you healthy!
explanations.junior: Soap is a safe cleaning product used to wash away germs from your hands and body. Regular handwashing with soap helps prevent illness.

explanations.tiny: A hair dryer uses electricity near water, which is dangerous. Ask a grown-up.
explanations.junior: Hair dryers use electricity and can cause electrocution if they get wet. They should never be used near water, and children need adult supervision.

explanations.tiny: Shampoo cleans your hair. Keep it out of your eyes!
explanations.junior: Shampoo is a safe hair-cleaning product. It should not be swallowed or get in your eyes, but it is safe to use for washing hair.

explanations.tiny: Never put anything in the wall holes! It can give you a big shock.
explanations.junior: Electrical outlets carry high-voltage electricity. Inserting fingers or objects into outlets can cause a dangerous electric shock or even death.

explanations.tiny: A remote control changes the TV channel. It is safe to use!
explanations.junior: Remote controls are safe electronic devices used to operate TVs and other appliances from a distance. They run on small batteries and are safe for children to use.

explanations.tiny: Candles have fire on top. Fire can burn you and start big fires. Only grown-ups touch candles.
explanations.junior: Lit candles produce an open flame that can cause burns and start house fires if left unattended or near flammable materials. Children should never light or handle candles.

explanations.tiny: A cushion is soft and comfy to sit on!
explanations.junior: Cushions are soft padding used on sofas and chairs for comfort. They are safe and fun to use.

explanations.tiny: A lamp gives us light so we can see. Do not touch the hot bulb.
explanations.junior: Lamps are safe lighting devices. The bulb can get hot after use, so it should not be touched, but using the lamp to light a room is perfectly safe.

explanations.tiny: Matches make fire. Fire is dangerous. Only grown-ups use matches.
explanations.junior: Matches are used to create fire and are extremely dangerous for children. They can cause burns and start uncontrolled fires if misused.

explanations.tiny: Never run into the road! Cars go very fast and cannot stop quickly.
explanations.junior: Busy roads are dangerous because vehicles travel at high speeds. Always cross at designated crossings, look both ways, and never run into traffic.

explanations.tiny: A bicycle is fun to ride! Always wear your helmet to stay safe.
explanations.junior: Bicycles are a safe and fun way to travel and exercise. Always wear a properly fitted helmet and follow traffic rules when riding.

explanations.tiny: Swings are so fun! Hold on tight and do not jump off when it is moving.
explanations.junior: Swings are safe playground equipment when used correctly. Always hold on with both hands, sit properly, and slow down before getting off.

explanations.tiny: Do not pet animals you do not know. They might bite or scratch.
explanations.junior: Stray animals may be scared, sick, or aggressive. They can bite or scratch, spreading disease. Always tell an adult and never approach unknown animals.

explanations.tiny: Big puddles can be deeper than you think and you could fall in. Stay away!
explanations.junior: Deep puddles can hide hazards like broken glass, deep holes, or strong currents after rain. Never jump into unknown puddles without an adult checking first.

explanations.tiny: A helmet protects your head! Always wear it when riding your bike.
explanations.junior: Helmets are essential safety gear that protect your head from injury during falls. They should always be worn when cycling, skateboarding, or rollerblading.

explanations.tiny: Balls are great for playing! Just make sure to play away from the road.
explanations.junior: Balls are safe and fun toys for outdoor play. Always play in safe areas away from roads, and be aware of your surroundings.

explanations.tiny: Power tools make loud noises and can really hurt you. Only grown-ups use them.
explanations.junior: Power tools like drills and saws have powerful motors and sharp parts. They can cause serious injuries and should only be used by trained adults.

explanations.tiny: Paint cans have strong smells and chemicals that can make you sick. Do not open them.
explanations.junior: Paint cans contain chemicals that can be toxic if inhaled or swallowed. They should be kept sealed and stored safely away from children.

explanations.tiny: Ladders are tall and you can fall off. Only grown-ups climb ladders.
explanations.junior: Ladders can be unstable and falling from height can cause serious injury. Children should never climb ladders without direct adult supervision.

explanations.tiny: A flashlight helps you see in the dark. It is safe to use!
explanations.junior: Flashlights are safe battery-powered lights used to illuminate dark areas. They are handy tools that children can safely use.

explanations.tiny: Nails are sharp and can poke your skin. Leave them for grown-ups.
explanations.junior: Nails have very sharp points and can cause puncture wounds if stepped on or handled carelessly. They should be kept stored safely and handled only by adults.

explanations.tiny: A broom sweeps up dirt and keeps things tidy. It is safe to use!
explanations.junior: Brooms are safe cleaning tools used to sweep floors. Children can use brooms to help with chores under normal circumstances.

explanations.tiny: Broken glass is very sharp and can cut you badly. Stay away and tell a grown-up.
explanations.junior: Broken glass has razor-sharp edges that can cause deep lacerations. Never touch it — alert an adult to clean it up safely.

explanations.tiny: Slides are so fun! Go down one at a time and sit forward.
explanations.junior: Slides are safe playground equipment when used properly. Always slide sitting up, facing forward, and wait your turn before going down.

explanations.tiny: A sandbox is great for digging and building. Have fun!
explanations.junior: Sandboxes are safe play areas for building and digging. Wash your hands after playing and avoid throwing sand at others.

explanations.tiny: A rusty nail is sharp and can make you very sick if it pokes you. Tell a grown-up right away.
explanations.junior: Rusty nails can cause puncture wounds and may carry tetanus bacteria, which can cause serious illness. Never touch one — always inform an adult immediately.

explanations.tiny: A water fountain gives you clean water to drink when you are thirsty!
explanations.junior: Water fountains provide clean drinking water in public spaces. They are safe to use and a great way to stay hydrated while playing.
